4.01.issue: Route /cic-pse OK. service_type_definitions: FPM,FS,GFX,KEC,RGC. KEC: true, FS: true. Fire Alarm + Sprinkler hardcoded. 0 schedules. 4 PSE safeguard types verified.

4.02.issue: Hood cleaning record: insert OK, readback verified. RLS allows CRUD. Cleanup done. No trigger to update location_service_schedules (separate table).

4.03.issue: Fire suppression record: insert OK, RLS OK, cleanup done. safeguard_type=fire_suppression (separate from hood_cleaning).

4.04.issue: Sprinkler record: insert OK, RLS OK, cleanup done. safeguard_type=sprinklers.

4.05.issue: Fire alarm record: insert OK, RLS OK, cleanup done. safeguard_type=fire_alarm.

4.06.issue: Overdue detection: client-side in usePSESchedules (diffDays < 0). Insert + cleanup OK. PSECoverageRiskWidget renders only when overdue items exist. No server-side alert generation.

4.07.issue: Route OK. documents insert+readback+cleanup OK. Storage bucket: NOT FOUND. AI analysis via SmartUploadModal. scan_status, ai_document_type, ai_confidence cols verified.

4.08.issue: Document categories are hardcoded (not from DB). Known: health_permit, fire_inspection, insurance_cert, business_license, training_cert, haccp_plan, food_handler_card, vendor_coi, other. DB documents with categories: none (empty table).

4.09.issue: Expiry tracking: expiration_date column OK. Inserted doc expiring tomorrow, readback OK, cleanup done. getDocStatus() returns "expiring" for ≤30 days. No server-side alert — display only.

4.10.issue: Route /vendor-connect OK. vendor_connect_profiles: exists. vendor_connect_spots: exists. Page skips fetch in demo mode. EvidLY-branded marketplace.

4.11.issue: Route /vendors OK. Vendor insert + readback + cleanup OK. RLS allows CRUD. Cols: name, contact_name, email, phone, category, status.

4.12.issue: Vendor service dates tracked in: location_service_schedules (vendor_name, next_due_date: OK), vendor_service_records (service_date, next_due_date: OK). Overdue flagged client-side via getServiceStatus().

4.13.issue: locations: 0 existing. Test insert + cleanup OK. Multi-location supported. /org-hierarchy manages tree view. /locations redirects to /org-hierarchy.

4.14.issue: Route OK. CA insert + full lifecycle OK (created→in_progress→completed). Cleanup done. Page UI is demo-only but DB table supports full workflow.

4.15.issue: Route /operations-check OK. 11 questions: 6 food safety + 5 fire/facility. Dual pillar: food_safety_score + facility_safety_score (separate). posture: categorical (critical/high/moderate/strong). NO blended score. Public route (no auth required). RLS: public insert, service_role read.

/cic-pse.note: PSE page always renders 4 safeguard cards. Empty cards show "No Record" status. KPI row shows current/due-soon/overdue/no-record counts.

/cic-pse (PSE gap).note: When no gaps: widget hidden (correct behavior — only surfaces when action needed). When overdue: red alert card with CTA to /facility-safety.

/documents.note: Empty state shows "No documents uploaded yet" with upload button CTA. Demo mode shows 30 sample documents with AI analysis.

/documents (filtering).note: Filter tabs show all categories. Empty categories show "No documents in this category" message.

/vendor-connect.note: Shows "No Vendor Connect partners yet" with "Apply to join" CTA when empty.

/vendors.note: EmptyState component with "Add your first vendor" guidance when no vendors. Uses AddVendorModal and InviteVendorModal.

/vendors (service dates).note: ServiceComplianceList shows service status per vendor. Empty state: "No service history" with guidance to schedule first service.

/org-hierarchy.note: OrgHierarchy page shows corporate→region→location tree. Add Location modal with county/jurisdiction matching. Demo shows 3 locations.

/corrective-actions.note: "No corrective actions on file" empty state with explanation of CA sources (temp deviations, checklist failures, inspections). Clear guidance on what generates CAs.

/operations-check.note: IRR is a lead-magnet page — always shows intake form → 11 questions (1/screen) → report. No empty state needed (self-contained flow).
